resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def reversePrefix(self, word: str, ch: str) -> str:
        if ch not in word:
            return word
        return word[:word.index(ch)+1][::-1] + word[word.index(ch)+1:]
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(n)

space complexity: O(1)

energy implications: LOW

explanation: This solution efficiently solves the problem by first checking if the character ch exists in the word. If it does not, the function returns the original word. If it does, the function uses Python's string slicing feature to extract the segment of the word that starts at the beginning and ends at the index of the first occurrence of ch (inclusive). It then reverses this segment using slicing with a step of -1 and concatenates it with the rest of the word.
